claims.text: The Flash Update on flash floods and landslides in Chattogram Division has been prepared by the Needs Assessment and Information Management Working Group and published on 09 July 2026, reporting 7 districts affected, 536,000 people affected, 11 deaths, 17,000 people temporarily displaced and 1,700 shelters activated.

Flash Update #2 (dated 11 July 2026, published 13 July) reports that 10 districts have been affected, more than 1.07 million people have been impacted, over 36,000 people remain temporarily displaced, and 26 casualties have been reported.

Situation Report #1 (dated 13 July 2026, published 14 July) reports 51 reported fatalities and more than 1.11 million people affected across 10 districts (Chattogram, Cox's Bazar, Bandarban, Khagrachhari, Rangamati, Moulvibazar, Sunamganj, Habiganj, Sylhet and Cumilla), with more than 38,000 people staying in over 1,000 evacuation shelters as of 12 July, and significant damage to homes, water systems, roads, bridges and public infrastructure.

Oxfam situation report on the Bangladesh flash floods (15 July 2026): 1.11 million people affected across 10 districts, 53 fatalities and 39 injuries reported as of 15 July; Chattogram district was hardest-hit, with Chattogram city recording 412.3mm of rain in 24 hours, the highest single-day July rainfall in 42 years.

BDRCS/IFRC nationwide situation report (14 July 2026): 1,074,534 people affected (NAWG figure) across 10 districts, about 38,500 people temporarily displaced (MoDMR figure) across 1,000+ shelters, 51 deaths, 267,918 households stranded, including 15 Rohingya refugee deaths reported by UNHCR among the Cox's Bazar camp toll.

BDRCS/IFRC nationwide situation report (posted 20 Jul 2026, originally published 19 Jul 2026): NAWG estimates 1,116,174 people affected across 10 districts (up from 1,074,534). MoDMR reports ~38,500 people temporarily displaced across 1,000+ shelters and 267,918 households stranded/isolated across seven districts (unchanged). At least 59 people have died nationwide (up from 53/51). In Cox's Bazar's 33 Rohingya refugee camps, flooding/landslides affected 28,000+ people, damaged 3,100+ shelters, and displaced 4,700+ individuals; UNHCR has confirmed 15 deaths among Rohingya refugees. As of 16 July only the Kushiyara River remained above danger level (at Markuli, Sunamganj and Fenchuganj, Sylhet), and water levels in the Surma (Sylhet/Sunamganj) and the Teesta, Dharla and Dudhkumar rivers (Rangpur Division) were falling.

Bangladesh Red Crescent Society Situation Report 4: Monsoon Flood & Landslide Response (posted 20 Jul 2026, originally published 17 Jul 2026), covering Chattogram, Cox's Bazar, Bandarban, Rangamati, Khagrachhari, Cumilla, Moulvibazar, Habiganj, Sunamganj and Sylhet. As of 16 July 9:00 AM, only the Kushiyara River remained above danger level (Markuli/Sunamganj and Fenchuganj/Sylhet stations); the Someshwari River at Kalmakanda had receded below danger level; Surma river conditions in Sylhet/Sunamganj were expected to improve within 24 hours; Teesta, Dharla and Dudhkumar river levels in Rangpur Division were falling and projected to fall further. BDRCS Emergency Operations Centre remains active.

Oxfam/CODEC Rapid Needs Assessment (RNA) Report on the Bangladesh Flash Flood 2026 (posted 21 Jul 2026): deep-dive assessment of Satkania and Banshkhali upazilas (Chattogram Division) found 360,000+ people affected in Satkania and 110,000+ in Banshkhali; 90-95% of surveyed households reported partial/complete loss of belongings; 92% of drinking water sources damaged/contaminated (94% in Banshkhali lacking safe water); all assessed latrines submerged; 90% of households reported insufficient food access and 59% reported signs of malnutrition.

description: Monsoon-triggered flash floods, riverine flooding and landslides across Chattogram Division and adjoining south-eastern/north-eastern Bangladesh districts (including Cox's Bazar, Bandarban, Rangamati, Khagrachhari, Cumilla, Moulvibazar, Habiganj, and, as the risk shifted north, Sylhet, Sunamganj and Rangpur), ongoing since early July 2026. As of 19-20 July 2026 (BDRCS/IFRC via ReliefWeb, and UNHCR), 1,116,174 people are affected across 10 districts, at least 59 people have died nationwide (including 15 Rohingya refugees per UNHCR), ~38,500 people remain temporarily displaced across 1,000+ shelters, and 267,918 households remain stranded. River levels in the previously at-risk northern districts (Sylhet, Sunamganj, Rangpur) were receding as of 16-19 July, with only the Kushiyara River still above danger level; response efforts are shifting toward early recovery even as emergency relief continues.

impact.summary: As of the 19-20 July 2026 BDRCS/IFRC situation report (the latest national-level figures found on ReliefWeb), the flooding/landslide event has affected 1,116,174 people across the same 10 districts (up from 1,074,534-1.11 million as of 14-15 July), with at least 59 deaths nationwide (up from 53), including 15 deaths among Rohingya refugees confirmed by UNHCR in Cox's Bazar camps. Temporary displacement (~38,500 people, 1,000+ shelters) and stranded households (267,918) are unchanged from the prior check. Hydrological conditions have begun improving: the flood risk that had been forecast to shift north into Sylhet, Sunamganj and Rangpur did materialize but rivers there (Surma, Teesta, Dharla, Dudhkumar) were receding by 16-19 July, with only the Kushiyara River still above danger level. Response is described as transitioning toward early recovery while relief operations continue; no authoritative source has declared the event resolved or contained.

location.region: Chattogram, Cox's Bazar, Bandarban, Rangamati, Khagrachhari, Moulvibazar, Sunamganj, Habiganj, Sylhet and Cumilla districts, southeastern Bangladesh

name: Monsoon Floods & Landslides — Chattogram Division, Bangladesh
